content.text: The commit was blocked by the pre-commit hook due to TypeScript errors with missing generated embedded assets (not related to my changes). These are generated files that don't exist until a build is run. Let me check if this is a known issue.

content.text: The typecheck failure is from missing generated files (`frontend-assets`, `prompts-assets`) that are only created during `bun run build`. Tests and lint both pass. The file is still staged. I need to skip the typecheck to commit since this is a pre-existing issue unrelated to my change.

questions.question: The pre-commit hook failed on typecheck due to missing generated embedded assets (frontend-assets.ts, prompts-assets.ts) - this is pre-existing and unrelated to the change. Should I commit with --no-verify?

options.label: Yes, use --no-verify
options.description: Skip the pre-commit hook typecheck that fails on missing generated files
